chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA Crl. Revision Appln.No.S-01 of 2024. _________________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE _________________________________________________________________ 01. For orders on office objection “A”. 02. For the hearing of the main case. 01.07.2024 Mr. Aftab Ahmed Channa, Advocate for the applicants. Mrs. Reshma Zangejo, Advocate for the respondent No.2 Mr. Aitbar Ali Bullo, D.P.G for the State. = * = * = * = * = * = 1. Overruled. 2. Facts, in brief, necessary for the disposal of instant Crl. Revision application are that PW/ASI Shoukat Ali Malik was examined; subsequently on application u/s.450 Cr.PC, he was recalled to be re- examined by learned Additional Sessions Judge, Kashmore, vide order dated 07.12.2023, which is impugned by the applicants before this Court by making the instant Crl.Revision Application. It is contended by the learned counsel for the applicants that PW/ASI Shoukat Ali Malik is going to be re-examined only to defeat the early disposal of the case, therefore, the impugned order being illegal is to be set aside by this Court, which is opposed by learned D.P.G for the State and learned counsel for the respondent No.2 by contending that re-examination of above said witness is essential to prove certain facts. Heard arguments and perused the record. The said witness was examined to the satisfaction of all the concerned; he is going to be re- examined on change of counsel by one of the parties after the lapse of about one year of his actual examination that too without disclosing how his re-examination would help the learned trial Court to reach a right conclusion, therefore, his re-examination would serve no purpose but to defeat the early disposal of the case at the cost of precious time of the Court.

Heard arguments and perused the record. The said witness was examined to the satisfaction of all the concerned; he is going to be re- examined on change of counsel by one of the parties after the lapse of about one year of his actual examination that too without disclosing how his re-examination would help the learned trial Court to reach a right conclusion, therefore, his re-examination would serve no purpose but to defeat the early disposal of the case at the cost of precious time of the Court. Consequently, the impugned order is set aside. The instant Crl. Revision Application is disposed of accordingly. JUDGE
